body:
## Rationale

Trivium's *Shogun* (2008) is widely considered their magnum opus — a 70-minute progressive thrash-metal album that topped metal publication year-end lists globally. Nick Augusto joined Trivium just before this album and it defines his technical peak with the band.

MetalForge has `nick-augusto-drum-setup` (generic kit article covering the In Waves/Vengeance Falls era) but ZERO album-specific articles for Trivium. Shogun opens the cluster.

**Search angles:** "trivium shogun drums", "nick augusto drum kit shogun", "trivium drummer 2008" — all unmet.

**Dedup:** `shogun-drum-setup` confirmed absent from albumArticles.js (grep 0). Not in any open seo-proposal.

---

## Spec

**Slug:** `shogun-drum-setup`  
**Drummer:** Nick Augusto  
**relatedDrummerSlug:** `nick-augusto`  
**Band:** Trivium  
**Album:** Shogun (2008)  
**Label:** Roadrunner Records  

### Gear (2008 era)
- **Kit:** DW Performance Series in Natural Lacquer — 22" kick, 10"/12"/14" rack toms, 16"/18" floor toms
- **Snare:** DW Edge 14×6.5 (power snare for Shogun's dense production)
- **Cymbals:** Meinl Byzance series (first major Trivium/Meinl pairing)
- **Hi-hats:** 14" Meinl Byzance Traditional
- **Crash:** 16"/18" Meinl Byzance Medium
- **Pedals:** DW 9000 Series
- **Sticks:** Vic Firth 5A American Classic

### Key editorial angles
- Shogun features the most technically demanding drum work of Trivium's catalog — odd-time signatures, 180+ BPM blast sections, extended jazz-influenced passages in "The Calamity" (9:55)
- DW's entry into the modern metalcore/prog-thrash space through Augusto
- Title track (11:39) is the longest Trivium song — features extended double kick patterns
- Meinl Byzance debut for the band — distinct from later Meinl Classics Custom period

### FAQ (3+ Q&A)
- Q: What drums did Nick Augusto use on Shogun?
- Q: Is Shogun the hardest Trivium album to play on drums?
- Q: What time signature is the Shogun title track?
- Q: Did Nick Augusto play all drums on the Shogun album?

### Internal links (≥3)
- → `/drummer/nick-augusto` (drummer profile)
- → `/articles/nick-augusto-drum-setup` (general kit guide)
- → `/articles/ashes-of-the-wake-drum-setup` (same era modern metal, different band)
- → `/genre/thrash-metal` (genre cross-link)

### Schema
- Article + BreadcrumbList + FAQPage JSON-LD

### LLM companion
- `public/llms/articles/shogun-drum-setup.md`

---

## Files to touch
- `packages/frontend/data/albumArticles.js` — add entry
- `public/llms/articles/shogun-drum-setup.md` — create LLM companion

---

## Verify
1. `grep 'shogun-drum-setup' packages/frontend/data/albumArticles.js` returns entry
2. `curl https://metalforge.io/articles/shogun-drum-setup` returns 200
3. Article + FAQPage JSON-LD present
4. `/llms/articles/shogun-drum-setup.md` returns 200
5. Title includes "Nick Augusto" + "Shogun" + "Drum Setup"

## Done
- [ ] Entry added to albumArticles.js
- [ ] LLM companion created
- [ ] Page live at /articles/shogun-drum-setup
title: SEO: Trivium 'Shogun' drum setup article (Nick Augusto, 2008 — opens Trivium album cluster, widely regarded as band's masterpiece)
